data.title: Australia shares likely to fall, NZ down
data.description:
Australian shares were likely to fall on Wednesday with probable losses in mining stocks on lower copper prices, while investors awaited quarterly production results from miner BHP due later in the day.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) fell 0.7%, a 34.4-point discount to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark closed unchanged on Tuesday.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index fell 0.2% in early trade.

data.title: Target Hospitality Shares Fall on $7M Secondary Offering
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Target Hospitality fell after the company said one of its shareholders would offer 7 million shares in an underwritten secondary offering.

Shares fell 9.9% to $14.32 in after-hours trading Tuesday, after closing up 1.9% at $15.90. At close, the stock had nearly doubled in value year to date.

Target Hospitality, which provides vertically integrated modular accommodations and hospitality service, said the shares were being offered by entities controlled by TDR Capital.

The company said it wasn't offering any shares and won't receive proceeds.

The underwriters for the offering have a 30-day option to buy an additional 1.05 million shares.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: GE Vernova Stock Has Soared to the Moon. How Earnings Could Keep It in Orbit. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

GE Vernova stock has been on an incredible tear, as power-hungry artificial intelligence data centers spread across America. That raises the bar for its coming earnings report.

The power generation equipment provider is set to report first-quarter numbers on Wednesday morning. Wall Street analysts tracked by FactSet are looking for earnings per share of $1.97 from sales of $9.2 billion. Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization, or Ebitda, is expected to be $770 million.

A year ago, GE Vernova reported earnings per share of $1.07 from sales of $8 billion, according to FactSet. Orders were $10.2 billion. Ebitda was $457 million.

Along with earnings, financial guidance should be updated, too. In January, GE Vernova management said they expect a 2026 Ebitda profit margin of about 12%, up from 8.4% in 2025. That implies Ebitda of about $5.3 billion. Wall Street, meanwhile, projects $5.8 billion.

For 2028, Ebitda margins should be about 20%, implying Ebitda of about $11.2 billion. Wall Street currently projects 2028 Ebitda closer to $11.9 billion. Things have simply gotten better-than-expected — and faster-than-expected: A year ago, the 2028 Ebitda estimate was closer to $7 billion.

Higher demand for power generation equipment and better pricing are responsible for much of the improvement. A lot of that is reflected in GE Vernova's share price.

Coming into the week, the stock has gained roughly 50% since the fourth-quarter earnings report and has climbed 15% since the fighting started in Iran.

The average analyst price target has gone to about $900 a share, up from $406 a year ago, while shares have race to roughly $1,000 from $290. The gains have left investors wondering how good can things get. More demand and higher pricing and expanded capacity could be coming on Wednesday.

How the stock will react is hard to say; shares rose 2.7% after the company reported fourth-quarter numbers in January. Options markets imply shares will move about 5% up or down after tomorrow's results.

Regardless of what happens, the Street is positive on the stock, with almost 80% of analysts who cover the company rating shares Buy.

"We believe GE Vernova's continued pricing momentum within gas power should continue to lead to strong order intake and margin expansion for years to come, increasing visibility into above-average growth and margins into the early-2030s," wrote JPMorgan analyst Mark Strouse in a preview report. He rates shares Buy and has an $1,150 price target for the stock.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Basis bids steady as showers forecast for crops
data.description:
Basis bids for hard red winter wheat were flat in a spot check of locations in the U.S. Plains states on Tuesday as farmers took stock of the impact of dry weather on their crops.

  • Dry weather in the U.S. Plains has begun to exact a toll on the wheat crop, although showers should provide some relief this weekend into early next week, according to Commodity Weather Group.

  • In a crop report released after the close of trading on Monday, the U.S. Department of Agriculture reported 30% of the nation's winter wheat is in good/excellent condition, down from 34% a week ago and down from 45% a year ago.

  • The K.C. May contract (KWK26) was last up 8-1/4 cents at $6.55-3/4 per bushel.

data.title: NIO Gives Its Model Y Nemesis LiDAR Vision — Wallet-Friendly Price Tag Unchanged
data.description:
Key points:
  • According to a report from the CnEVPost, the new Onvo L90 introduces a LiDAR sensor for the first time to enhance assisted driving capabilities in addition to other software and hardware updates.
  • Deliveries of the updated vehicle are slated to start on May 9.
  • Onvo has delivered about 50,000 units of the three-row SUV vehicles as of early-April.

Chinese EV maker Nio Inc (NIO) on Tuesday launched the updated version of its Onvo L90 SUV which has seen significant demand after its initial version was launched last year.

The 2026 model year Onvo L90 starts at RMB 265,800 ($38,964), including the battery, same as its model year 2025 version. If customers choose to rent the battery instead, they can purchase the vehicle for RMB 179,800. In comparison, Tesla’s mass market three row SUV available in China, called the Model Y L, starts at RMB 339,000.

According to a report from the CnEVPost, the new variant introduces a LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) sensor for the first time to enhance assisted driving capabilities in addition to other software and hardware updates. Deliveries of the updated vehicle are slated to start on May 9.

Meanwhile, Tesla CEO Elon Musk has often dismissed the use of LiDAR for assisted driving, opining that camera or vision-only inputs can serve the purpose with efficiency.

Nio’s Onvo Success

Nio launched Onvo as a sub-brand catering to the mass market in 2024 unlike its namesake brand which makes luxury EVs. The company’s first vehicle was the L60, followed by the L90 which was launched in July 2025. Onvo has delivered about 50,000 units of the three-row SUV vehicles as of early-April.

Nio is now looking forward to launching its third Onvo brand vehicle, called the L80, in the second quarter. L80 is a five-seat SUV unlike the L90 which is targeted at larger families.

“With an expanding product lineup and continuous product upgrades, the ONVO brand will deliver the finest mobility experiences to more family users, driving steady sales growth,” Nio CEO William Li said in March .

How Did Retail Traders React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around NIO stock stayed within the ‘neutral’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume remained at ‘high’ levels. Retail chatter around NIO has risen 162% over the past day, platform data shows.

A user opined that high gas prices would spur Nio’s EV sales in China, terming NIO stock a “screaming buy.”

https://www.stocktwits.com/Captiva7/message/650948678

Another voiced hope for positive announcements from the company turning the stock back into the green territory after it closed down 3% on Tuesday.

https://www.stocktwits.com/NIOconversationArp2023/message/650940863

According to data from Koyfin, 16 of the 25 analysts covering NIO rate it ‘Buy’ or higher while seven rate it Hold and 2 rate it Sell or Strong Sell. The 12-month average price target on the stock is $6.58, representing a potential upside of about 2% from last close.

NIO stock has gained about 79% over the past 12 months.

Exchange Rate: RMB1= $0.15
